# Working with Macro Time Series {#sec-time-series}

Macroeconomic data is time series data, and it comes with its own set of practical challenges: mixed frequencies, seasonal patterns, real versus nominal values, data revisions, and rebasing.

This chapter covers the essential data wrangling operations you will use in every subsequent chapter.

## Date handling and frequency conversion

## Seasonal adjustment

## Real versus nominal: adjusting for inflation

```{r}
#| eval: false
library(inflateR)
```

## Rebasing indices

## Calculating growth rates

## Handling data revisions

## Exercises

1. Download monthly UK CPI data and quarterly GDP data. Convert CPI to quarterly frequency by averaging. Merge the two series.

2. Using `inflateR`, convert a series of nominal UK GDP values to real terms (base year 2019). How does the growth profile change?

3. Rebase the ECB's HICP index to January 2015 = 100. Plot the rebased series for Germany and France.
